title: Mae Hong Son province

infobox.value: แม่ฮ่องสอน

infobox.key: This article contains [Lanna text](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language").
infobox.value: Without proper [rendering support](/wiki/Help:Multilingual_support_(East_Asian)#Archaic_Hangul "Help:Multilingual support (East Asian)"), you may see [question marks, boxes, or other symbols](/wiki/Specials_(Unicode_block)#Replacement_character "Specials (Unicode block)") instead of [Tai Tham script](/wiki/Tai_Tham_script "Tai Tham script").

[Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): แม่ฮ่องสอน, pronounced [[mɛ̂ː.hɔ̂ŋ.sɔ̌ːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"), ([Northern Thai](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language"): ᨾᩯ᩵ᩁᩬ᩵ᨦᩈᩬᩁ, [Burmese](/wiki/Burmese_language "Burmese language"): မဲဟောင်ဆောင်, [Shan](/wiki/Shan_language "Shan language"): မႄႈႁွင်ႈသွၼ်), formerly called *Mae Rong Son*), also spelled *Maehongson*, *Mae Hong Sorn* or *Maehongsorn*, is one of Thailand's seventy-six [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*changwat*). It lies in [upper northern Thailand](/wiki/Northern_Thailand#Regional_classification_of_northern_Thailand "Northern Thailand") and is the westernmost province. Neighboring provinces are (clockwise from north) [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"), [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") and [Tak](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province"). To the west, the province borders [Kayin State](/wiki/Kayin_State "Kayin State") and [Kayah State](/wiki/Kayah_State "Kayah State") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar").

was ceded to [Siam](/wiki/Siam "Siam"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) but the adjacent Möngmaü and Mehsakun trans-[Salween](/wiki/Salween "Salween") districts also claimed by [Siam](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") (as territories on the eastern side of the [Salween](/wiki/Salween_River "Salween River") River), were kept as part of [British Burma](/wiki/British_Burma "British Burma").[[8]](#cite_note-IGI-8)

**This article contains [Lanna text](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language").** Without proper [rendering support](/wiki/Help:Multilingual_support_(East_Asian)#Archaic_Hangul "Help:Multilingual support (East Asian)"), you may see [question marks, boxes, or other symbols](/wiki/Specials_(Unicode_block)#Replacement_character "Specials (Unicode block)") instead of [Tai Tham script](/wiki/Tai_Tham_script "Tai Tham script").

**Mae Hong Son province** [Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): แม่ฮ่องสอน, pronounced [[mɛ̂ː.hɔ̂ŋ.sɔ̌ːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"), ([Northern Thai](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language"): ᨾᩯ᩵ᩁᩬ᩵ᨦᩈᩬᩁ, [Burmese](/wiki/Burmese_language "Burmese language"): မဲဟောင်ဆောင်, [Shan](/wiki/Shan_language "Shan language"): မႄႈႁွင်ႈသွၼ်), formerly called *Mae Rong Son*), also spelled *Maehongson*, *Mae Hong Sorn* or *Maehongsorn*, is one of Thailand's seventy-six [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*changwat*). It lies in [upper northern Thailand](/wiki/Northern_Thailand#Regional_classification_of_northern_Thailand "Northern Thailand") and is the westernmost province. Neighboring provinces are (clockwise from north) [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"), [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") and [Tak](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province"). To the west, the province borders [Kayin State](/wiki/Kayin_State "Kayin State") and [Kayah State](/wiki/Kayah_State "Kayah State") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar").
Mae Hong Son's nickname is "the city of three mists". It is hemmed in by the high [mountain ranges](/wiki/Mountain_range "Mountain range") of the [Shan Hills](/wiki/Shan_Hills "Shan Hills") and is the most mountainous province in Thailand, occupying 12,765 square kilometres (4,929 sq mi).[[1]](#cite_note-AREA-1) The province is often covered with mist. Mae Hong Son town was originally established in the early 19th century as an elephant training camp as ordered by the then King of [Chiang Mai.](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province")
As of 2012[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit), Mae Hong Son was the poorest province in [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand").[[5]](#cite_note-nation-20140923-5)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

**Mae Hong Son province** was formerly part of [Mawkmai State](/wiki/Mawkmai_State "Mawkmai State"), one of the [Shan States](/wiki/Shan_States "Shan States") which had been founded in 1767 by [Hsai Khiao](/w/index.php?title=Hsai_Khiao&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hsai Khiao (page does not exist)"), from a noble family of [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

As a result of the [Anglo-Siamese Boundary Commission](/w/index.php?title=Anglo-Siamese_Boundary_Commission&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anglo-Siamese Boundary Commission (page does not exist)") of 1892–1893, **Mae Hong Son district** was ceded to [Siam](/wiki/Siam "Siam"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) but the adjacent Möngmaü and Mehsakun trans-[Salween](/wiki/Salween "Salween") districts also claimed by [Siam](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") (as territories on the eastern side of the [Salween](/wiki/Salween_River "Salween River") River), were kept as part of [British Burma](/wiki/British_Burma "British Burma").[[8]](#cite_note-IGI-8)

## Environment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Environment")]

The [Thai government](/wiki/Government_of_Thailand "Government of Thailand"), to solve persistent water shortages in the [central region](/wiki/Central_Thailand "Central Thailand"), have proposed a 70.7 billion [baht](/wiki/Thai_baht "Thai baht") plan to divert some 1.8 billion m3 of water annually from the [Yuam River](/wiki/Yuam_River "Yuam River") to the perennially underfilled [Bhumibol Dam](/wiki/Bhumibol_Dam "Bhumibol Dam"). Several of the province's districts would impacted. As part of the plan, the [Royal Irrigation Department](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Irrigation_Department&action=edit&redlink=1 "Royal Irrigation Department (page does not exist)") (RID), would build 69 metre high dam with a storage capacity of 68.7 million m3 constructed on 2,075 [rai](/wiki/Rai_(unit) "Rai (unit)") of forest land, together with a pumping station on a separate 55 rai plot and a 61.5 kilometre-long tunnel passing through 14 villages. The inhabitants of the area—primarily [Karen tribes people](/wiki/Karen_people "Karen people")—largely oppose the project. In December 2019, the RID submitted a second [environmental impact assessment](/wiki/Environmental_impact_assessment "Environmental impact assessment") (EIA) to the [Office of the Natural Resources and Environmental Policy and Planning](/wiki/Ministry_of_Natural_Resources_and_Environment_(Thailand) "Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment (Thailand)") (ONEP). ONEP rejected the EIA for the second time due to concerns about forest destruction, [tunnel rock waste](/wiki/Tunnel_rock_recycling "Tunnel rock recycling"), and compensation issues.[[9]](#cite_note-BP-20200407-9)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

### Ethnic diversity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ethnic diversity")]

[Shan people](/wiki/Shan_people "Shan people") is the largest population group in the province. They migrated from [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State") in [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"). Further there are seven [hill tribes](/wiki/Hill_tribe_(Thailand) "Hill tribe (Thailand)") which consists of 63% of the provincial population: [Karen](/wiki/Karen_people "Karen people"), [Lahu](/wiki/Lahu_people "Lahu people"), [Lisu](/wiki/Lisu_people "Lisu people"), [Lua](/wiki/Lua_people "Lua people"), [Miao](/wiki/Miao_people "Miao people"), [Chinese Yunnan](/wiki/Chin_Haw "Chin Haw") and [Pa-O](/wiki/Pa%27O_people "Pa'O people") people.

The Karen are not a single group, but rather a mix of closely related tribes. Among the smallest of the Karen tribes in Thailand are [Kayan Lahwi](/wiki/Kayan_Lahwi "Kayan Lahwi"). This group's women are recognized by the large brass rings they wear around their necks. Population density is 22 persons per km2, the lowest of all [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Language")]

* [Tai Yai](/wiki/Shan_people "Shan people") people speak [their own language](/wiki/Shan_language "Shan language"). The written language with round alphabets is like Mon and Burmese script.
* [Karen](/wiki/Karen_people "Karen people") and [Chinese Haw](/wiki/Chin_Haw "Chin Haw") have their own spoken and written language.
* [Lahu](/wiki/Lahu_people "Lahu people"), [Lisu](/wiki/Lisu_people "Lisu people") and [Lua people](/wiki/Lua_people "Lua people") have their own spoken language, but their written language uses the [Latin alphabet](/wiki/Latin_alphabet "Latin alphabet").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Religion")]

Most inhabitants, 77.5%, are [Buddhists](/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism"), followed by [Christians](/wiki/Christians "Christians") at 20.9%, and [Muslims](/wiki/Muslim "Muslim") at 0.5%. About 1.1% believe in [animism](/wiki/Tai_folk_religion "Tai folk religion") and other religions.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Geography")]

### Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Location")]

**Mae Hong Son province** is approximately 924 kilometres (574 mi) north of [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok") by road. To the north and west it connects to a total of three states in the [Union of Burma](/wiki/Union_of_Burma "Union of Burma"), namely the southern portion of [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State"), [Kayah State](/wiki/Kayah_State "Kayah State"), and [Kawthoolei State](/wiki/Kawthoolei "Kawthoolei"), via the [Dawna Range](/wiki/Dawna_Range "Dawna Range"), and the [Salween](/wiki/Salween "Salween") and [Moei](/wiki/Moei_River "Moei River") Rivers. These formations serve as natural boundaries between the countries. To the south, it borders the district of [Tha Song Yang](/wiki/Tha_Song_Yang_District "Tha Song Yang District") and Tak, via the rivers [Yuam](/wiki/Yuam_River "Yuam River") and [Ngao](/wiki/Ngao_River "Ngao River"), which serve as a provincial boundary. To the east it borders the districts of [Wiang Haeng](/wiki/Wiang_Haeng_District "Wiang Haeng District"), [Chiang Dao](/wiki/Chiang_Dao_District "Chiang Dao District"), [Mae Taeng](/wiki/Mae_Taeng_District "Mae Taeng District"), [Mae Chaem](/wiki/Mae_Chaem_District "Mae Chaem District"), [Hot](/wiki/Hot_District "Hot District") and [Omkoi](/wiki/Omkoi_District "Omkoi District") in [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") province, via the [central](/wiki/Central_Thanon_Thongchai_Mountains "Central Thanon Thongchai Mountains") and [east](/wiki/East_Thanon_Thongchai_Mountains "East Thanon Thongchai Mountains") sections of the [Thanon Thongchai](/wiki/Thanon_Thongchai_Mountains "Thanon Thongchai Mountains") mountain ranges, which serve as a boundary between the two provinces.

Every district in **Mae Hong Son** province shares a common border, measuring approximately 483 kilometres in total length, with [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"). The common border consists of approximately 326 km (203 mi) of land boundary and 157 km (98 mi) of river boundary (not counting the [Salween](/wiki/Salween_River "Salween River"), 127 km (79 mi), and [Moei](/wiki/Moei_River "Moei River"), 30 km (19 mi)).[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### Topography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Topography")]

Most of the areas of **Mae Hong Son province** are the complex mountain ranges of the [Thai highlands](/wiki/Thai_highlands "Thai highlands"), parts of which are still covered with [rainforest](/wiki/Rain_forest "Rain forest"). The total forest area is 10,915 km2 (4,214 sq mi) or 85.5 percent of provincial area.[[1]](#cite_note-AREA-1)

The [Daen Lao Range](/wiki/Daen_Lao_Range "Daen Lao Range"), in the northernmost portion of the province, marks the northern boundary between [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") and [Burma](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"), while the [Dawna Range](/wiki/Dawna_Range "Dawna Range") in the west serves as the boundary between [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") and [Burma](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar"). The [Thanon Thongchai Range](/wiki/Thanon_Thongchai_Range "Thanon Thongchai Range") in the east of the province serves as the boundary between the provinces of **Mae Hong Son** and [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province"). The highest point of the province is [Doi Mae Ya](/w/index.php?title=Doi_Mae_Ya&action=edit&redlink=1 "Doi Mae Ya (page does not exist)") (ยอดเขาแม่ยะ), in the [Pai District](/wiki/Pai_District "Pai District") in the province's northeast, at 2,005 metres (6,578 ft) elevation.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Climate")]

**Mae Hong Son province** has a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") ([Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") category Aw). Winters are dry and warm. Temperatures rise until May. [Monsoon season](/wiki/Wet_season "Wet season") runs from May through October, with heavy rain and somewhat cooler temperatures during the day, although nights remain warm. Temperature statistics: Its maximum temperature is 44.6 °C (112.3 °F) on 28 April 2016 and the lowest temperature is 3.9 °C (39.0 °F). The highest average temperature is 35.6 °C (96.1 °F) on 25 December 1999 and the minimum average temperature is 18.0 °C (64.4 °F). The average relative humidity is 96.99% and the minimum relative humidity is 20%. Annual rainfall is 1,064.9 millimeters and on 23 April 1968 was for 24 hour the rainfall 130.4 mm. The number of rainy days was 130 days for a year.[[15]](#cite_note-15)  
[Weather box Mae Hong Son](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son#Climate "Mae Hong Son") and [Weather box Mae Sariang](/wiki/Mae_Sariang#Climate "Mae Sariang")

### Water resources

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Water resources")]

There are 31 water reservoirs with total capacity of 12.31 million cubic meters:

* Huai So Tue, capacity 1,880,000 m3
* Huai Fai Kho, capacity 950,000 m3
* Huai Mae Hong Son, cap. 734,000 m3.
* Huai Mae Salap, cap. 700,000 m3.

There are 617 groundwater wells, which are used to mine groundwater for general use.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Mineral resources

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Mineral resources")]

Among the minerals that have been discovered in the province are [limestone](/wiki/Limestone "Limestone"), [coal](/wiki/Coal "Coal"), [lignite](/wiki/Lignite "Lignite"), [lead](/wiki/Lead "Lead"), [zinc](/wiki/Zinc "Zinc"), [tin](/wiki/Tin "Tin"), [tungsten](/wiki/Tungsten "Tungsten"), [antimony](/wiki/Antimony "Antimony"), [fluoride](/wiki/Fluoride "Fluoride"), and [feldspar](/wiki/Feldspar "Feldspar"). The mineral area is approximately 2,440 km2 (19% of provincial area). One of those mines in Mae Hong Son province is the Samoeng mine.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### Provincial government

The province is divided into seven districts (*[amphoes](/wiki/Amphoe "Amphoe")*). These are further divided into 45 subdistricts (*[tambons](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")*) and 415 villages (*[mubans](/wiki/Muban "Muban")*).[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Local government")]

As of 26 November 2019 there are:[[20]](#cite_note-20) one [Mae Hong Son Provincial Administration Organisation](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_Provincial_Administration_Organisation&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mae Hong Son Provincial Administration Organisation (page does not exist)") (**ongkan borihan suan changwat**) and seven municipal (*[thesaban](/wiki/Thesaban "Thesaban")*) areas in the province. [Mae Hong Son](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son "Mae Hong Son") has town (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")*) status. There are a further six subdistrict [municipalities](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") (*[thesaban tambon](/wiki/Thesaban_tambon "Thesaban tambon")*). The non-municipal areas are administered by 42 Subdistrict Administrative Organisations (SAO) (*ongkan borihan suan tambon*).

## Symbols

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Symbols")]

The [provincial seal](/wiki/Seals_of_the_provinces_of_Thailand "Seals of the provinces of Thailand"), *Rup chang nai thong nam* (รูปช้างในท้องน้ำ), meaning 'image of an elephant in a body of water', is a reference to the training of wild elephants for battle and various types of animal labour. "Rup chang nai thong nam" was selected as the provincial seal because it refers to Mae Hong Son's founding, which began with Lord Kaeo of Ma being sent to capture elephants for the Lord of Chiang Mai (1825–1846). Once in Mae Hong Son, he gathered the scattered Shan settlements to establish two main villages, Ban Pang Mu and Ban Mae Hong Son, that would be ruled by their elected leaders.

The provincial flag is horizontally divided in dark brown/blue/dark brown (1:3:1) with the image of the provincial seal in the middle of the flag.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

The [provincial tree](/wiki/List_of_Thai_provincial_trees "List of Thai provincial trees") is the *[Millettia brandisiana](/wiki/Millettia "Millettia")* ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กระพี้จั่น; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **kraphichan**).

The provincial flower is the [Tree marigold](/wiki/Tithonia_diversifolia "Tithonia diversifolia") ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): บัวตอง; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **buatong**).

The provincial animal is the [Blyth's river frog](/wiki/Blyth%27s_river_frog "Blyth's river frog") ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กบภูเขา; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **kobphukhao**).

The official provincial slogan promoted by the Thai government is หมอกสามฤดู กองมูเสียดฟ้า ป่าเขียวขจี ผู้คนดี ประเพณีงาม ลือนามถิ่นบัวตอง
([RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **mok sam ruedu, kong mu siat fa, pa khiao khachi, phu khon di, prapheni ngam, lue nam thin bua tong**) 'Mists throughout the three seasons, the Kong Mu (monastery) that scrapes the sky, verdant forests, gentle people, beautiful customs; renowned land of [marigolds](/wiki/Tithonia_diversifolia "Tithonia diversifolia")'.

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

### Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Education")]

Higher educational institutions are:

* [Chiang Mai Rajabhat University](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_Rajabhat_University "Chiang Mai Rajabhat University"), **Mae Hong Son** campus.
* **Mae Hong Son** Community College.
* [Nawamintrachinaracha Industrial and Community Education College.](/w/index.php?title=Nawamintrachinaracha_Industrial_and_Community_Education_College.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nawamintrachinaracha Industrial and Community Education College. (page does not exist)")
* [Mae Sariang Industrial and Community Education College.](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sariang_Industrial_and_Community_Education_College.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mae Sariang Industrial and Community Education College. (page does not exist)")

There are eight secondary schools in **Mae Hong Son**.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### Healthcare

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Healthcare")]

Every district has a hospital. There are also 72 subdistrict health-promoting hospitals. There are 63 public health centers, 10 insect-induced disease control centers, 15 malaria clinics, 21 community [malaria](/wiki/Malaria "Malaria") centers and one health service ([Thanyarak Mae Hong Son hospital](/w/index.php?title=Thanyarak_Mae_Hong_Son_hospital&action=edit&redlink=1 "Thanyarak Mae Hong Son hospital (page does not exist)")).[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Transportation")]

#### Roads

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Roads")]

There are four major roads in the province:[[24]](#cite_note-24)

* [Hot District](/wiki/Hot_district "Hot district") of [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") to **Mae Hong Son**, distance 345 km
* [Mae Tang](/wiki/Mae_Taeng_district "Mae Taeng district") District of [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") province to **Mae Hong Son**, distance 246 km.
* [Mae Sot](/wiki/Mae_Sot_district "Mae Sot district") District of [Tak province](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province") to **Mae Hong Son**.
* [Mae Chaem](/wiki/Mae_Chaem_district "Mae Chaem district") District of [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") province to [Khun Yuam District](/wiki/Khun_Yuam_district "Khun Yuam district") of **Mae Hong Son** province, distance 90 km.

#### Public transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Public transport")]

Two bus companies ([Prem Pracha Transport Co](/w/index.php?title=Prem_Pracha_Transport_Co&action=edit&redlink=1 "Prem Pracha Transport Co (page does not exist)") and [Sombat Tour Co](/w/index.php?title=Sombat_Tour_Co&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sombat Tour Co (page does not exist)").) operate four bus services:[[25]](#cite_note-25)

* [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok") - [Mae Hong Son](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son "Mae Hong Son"), distance is 928 km, travel time is 17 hours.
* [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai "Chiang Mai") - [Mae Sariang](/wiki/Mae_Sariang_district "Mae Sariang district") - **Mae Hong Son**, distance is 359 km, travel time is eight hours.
* [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") ([Pai District](/wiki/Pai_district "Pai district")) - **Mae Hong Son**, distance is 250 km, travel time is six hours.
* [Mae Sot District](/wiki/Mae_Sot_district "Mae Sot district") of [Tak province](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province") - [Mae Sariang District](/wiki/Mae_Sariang_district "Mae Sariang district") of **Mae Hong Son.**

#### Air transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Air transport")]

[Bangkok Airways](/wiki/Bangkok_Airways "Bangkok Airways") provides daily flights between [Chiang Mai International Airport](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_International_Airport "Chiang Mai International Airport") and [Mae Hong Son Airport](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son_Airport "Mae Hong Son Airport").  
[Kan Air](/wiki/Kan_Air "Kan Air") provides flights, three times a week, between [Chiang Mai International Airport](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_International_Airport "Chiang Mai International Airport") and [Pai Airport](/wiki/Pai_Airport "Pai Airport").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### Electricity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Electricity")]

**Mae Hong Son** is supplied with electricity from power stations in [Chiang Mai](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") province (districts [Chom Thong](/wiki/Chom_Thong_district,_Chiang_Mai "Chom Thong district, Chiang Mai"), [Hot](/wiki/Hot_district "Hot district") and [Mae Taeng](/wiki/Mae_Taeng_district "Mae Taeng district")), along with eight power plants in **Mae Hong Son province**

* [Pha Bong Dam Hydro Plant](/w/index.php?title=Pha_Bong_Dam_Hydro_Plant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pha Bong Dam Hydro Plant (page does not exist)")
* [Mae Sa Nga Dam Hydro Plant](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sa_Nga_Dam_Hydro_Plant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mae Sa Nga Dam Hydro Plant (page does not exist)")
* [Mae Sariang Hydro Plant](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sariang_Hydro_Plant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mae Sariang Hydro Plant (page does not exist)")
* [Mae Hong Son Diesel Power Plant](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_Diesel_Power_Plant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mae Hong Son Diesel Power Plant (page does not exist)")
* [Pha Bong Solar Power Plant](/w/index.php?title=Pha_Bong_Solar_Power_Plant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pha Bong Solar Power Plant (page does not exist)")

In 2020, out of 123,826 households only 77,402 households have electricity.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

### Communications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Hong_Son_province&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Communications")]

As of 2018 there were 65,153 households, of which 2.1% used [telephones](/wiki/Telephone "Telephone"), 15.2% used [computers](/wiki/Computer "Computer") with no [internet connection](/wiki/Internet_access "Internet access"), and 45.5% used [computers](/wiki/Computer "Computer") with [internet](/wiki/Internet "Internet"). As of 2018 there were 182,615 people aged six years and older, of which 22.2% used [computers](/wiki/Computer "Computer"), 38.9% the [internet](/wiki/Internet "Internet"), and 81.3% used [mobile phones](/wiki/Mobile_phone "Mobile phone").[[28]](#cite_note-28)
